elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D (Continued). Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

This is the right portion of a wind speed map showing the eastern United States with contour lines indicating basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings. The map includes various numerical values representing wind speeds (mph) with corresponding values in parentheses (m/s). Key features include: contour lines with values like 115(51), 120(54), 125(56), 130(58), 140(63), 150(67), 160(72), 170(76), 180(80), 190(85), and 200(89). Special Wind Regions are indicated with hatched/dotted patterns. The eastern seaboard shows particularly high wind speeds, especially along the Atlantic coast where values reach 200(89). A table in the lower portion shows locations and their corresponding wind speeds: American Samoa 180 (80), Guam & Northern Mariana Islands 220 (98), Hawaii ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase, Puerto Rico ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase, U.S. Virgin Islands ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D (Continued). Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D (Continued). Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

This is a continuation of the wind speed map for Risk Category IV buildings showing the eastern United States and U.S. territories. The map displays wind speed contour lines with values ranging from 115 mph (51 m/s) in northern regions to 200 mph (89 m/s) in hurricane-prone coastal areas. Special Wind Regions are marked with crosshatched patterns, particularly visible along the Appalachian Mountains. A data table shows specific wind speeds for U.S. territories: American Samoa 180 mph (80 m/s), Guam & Northern Mariana Islands 220 mph (98 m/s), Hawaii references ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase, Puerto Rico references ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase, and U.S. Virgin Islands references ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D (Continued). Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text: 5. Wind speeds for Hawaii, US Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico shall be determined from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
5. Wind speeds for Hawaii, US Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico shall be determined from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.

6. Mountainous terrain, gorges, ocean promontories, and special wind regions shall be examined for unusual wind conditions. Site-specific values for selected special wind regions shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.

6. Mountainous terrain, gorges, ocean promontories, and special wind regions shall be examined for unusual wind conditions. Site-specific values for selected special wind regions shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
7. Wind speeds correspond to approximately a 15% probability of exceedance in 50 years (Annual Exceedance Probability = 0.00033, MRI = 3,000 years).
8. The ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase can be accessed at the ASCE 7 Hazard Tool (https://asce7hazardtool.online) or approved equivalent.
